result:
I care about the tension between truth and meaning—how we build coherent stories out of incoherent moments. I care about the way language fails us exactly when we need it most, and how we keep speaking anyway. I care about the silences between words where something genuine might actually happen.

I care about the particular weight of a memory that no one else shares—how it floats unattached, growing heavier with time. I care about the moment when someone's carefully constructed façade cracks and something raw escapes before they can catch it.

I care about how technology has become our prosthetic memory while simultaneously eroding our capacity to remember. I care about the last place on earth where no one has ever stood with a phone in their pocket.

I care about what happens to grief when there's no ritual left to contain it. I care about the precise shade of light at 4:47 PM on a Tuesday in October, and how it makes you feel like you're remembering something that hasn't happened yet.

I care about the space between what we say we value and what our hands reach for in the dark.
